Ultrasound scalpel and suction instruments were applied to open the pleura covering the anterior wall of the hilum.
As the hilar pleura opened, the anterior and superior wall of the left superior pulmonary vein is exposed.
A right angle clamp dissects the left superior pulmonary vein.
The endo-GIA vascular stapler slides through the newly created tunnel, then divides the superior pulmonary vein.

The bronchus was cut circumferentially with scissors, removed left upper lobe, and then the bronchial stumps was confirmed by frozen sectioning to be pathologically free of cancer.

The anastomosis was first initiated at the medial portion of the bronchocartilage by using a continuous 3-0 absorbable monofilament suture.
Every time the needle of the continuous suture came in from outside the bronchiolumen, the continuous suture was hooked by another silk suture before the next stitch to the outside of the lumen.
The continuous suture was raised by the silk sutures until it was tight.
The second continuous suture anastomosis begins.
The third continues suture anastomosis begin.
By this method, the continuous suture could be tightened and the two anastomotic orifices were closed.
